summary:
Phosphoglucomutase converts glucose-1-phosphate into glucose-6-phosphate essential for both glycogen synthesis and breakdown; this makes it unique among candidate enzymes as being shared by both pathways. The ability to switch phosphate groups allows regulation over whether more glucoses are added to form glycogen or released through its hydrolysis process. \boxed{Phosphoglucomutase}

The summary captures the essence of why phosphoglucomutase serves as an integral part across both metabolic routes without extraneous details while focusing solely on clinically significant aspects related to answering the posed query about their intersectional functionality within glycolytic mechanisms involving glycogen metabolism specifically. 

Key Points Covered:

* Role-specific function bridging two opposing yet interrelated biochemical reactions
* Specificity towards facilitating crucial step(s)
* Clinical relevance regarding regulatory control over carbohydrate storage/utilisation dynamics
